title: QA-10.2 Critical Widths threshold allowlist
description: Schema for tests/audit/threshold-significance.json — the allowlist of CSS media-query thresholds that QA-10.2 must tolerate as non-regressions. See tests/audit/THRESHOLD-ALLOWLIST.md for lifecycle rules and PDR-007 § Constraints 5 for the driving decision.

$schema.description: Reference to this schema file (tooling hint; not interpreted by QA-10.2).

allowlist.description: Thresholds (in px) that QA-10.2 should classify as allowed rather than unhandled. Empty by default; entries are added via PR as allowlistable thresholds emerge.

threshold_px.description: The CSS media-query threshold value in px (em/rem normalized to px at 16px root).

reason.description: Why this threshold is allowlisted rather than promoted to the QA-09 width set or removed from the CSS. One sentence is usually enough; name the source (e.g., third-party reset, intentional outlier, typo-under-repair).

source_file.description: The built CSS file containing the @media rule that produces this threshold (e.g., dist/_astro/_id_.DamDMsb6.css). The hashed filename is fine — it documents provenance at the time of addition.

added.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the entry was added. Must be the PR merge day or the PR author's working date; back-dating is not permitted.

review_by.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the entry expires. Convention is `added` + 90 days. QA-10.2 fails when today > review_by; the reviewer then extends, promotes to QA-09 widths, or removes the entry (and fixes the CSS).
